content:
Key Findings

  • West Lancashire Borough Council spent £332.9M across 43,063 transactions between 2016/17 and 2025/26
  • Total spending increased by £18.1M (62.9%) from 2017 to 2025
  • The largest year-over-year increase was £29.9M (118.9%) from 2019 to 2020
  • The largest year-over-year decrease was £25.5M (55.4%) from 2021 to 2022
  • The council worked with 4,614 unique suppliers over the 10-year period

Supplier Retention Patterns

West Lancashire Borough Council has worked with 4,614 unique suppliers over the past decade. This large number suggests a high level of supplier churn, with many vendors appearing only once in the spending records. The council's spending patterns show significant fluctuations year-over-year, which may contribute to this turnover. For instance, the £29.9M increase from 2019 to 2020 likely required new suppliers to meet demand, while the £25.5M decrease from 2021 to 2022 may have led to reduced contracts with existing vendors.

The high number of unique suppliers also raises questions about the council's procurement strategy. While working with a diverse range of suppliers can promote competition and innovation, it can also increase administrative costs and reduce economies of scale. The council may benefit from analysing its supplier retention rates and identifying opportunities to build longer-term partnerships with key vendors.

Year-over-Year Spending Trends

West Lancashire Borough Council's spending has seen significant fluctuations over the past decade. The largest increase occurred between 2019 and 2020, with spending rising by £29.9M (118.9%). This substantial growth may have been driven by increased demand for services or one-off projects. Conversely, the largest decrease occurred between 2021 and 2022, with spending falling by £25.5M (55.4%). This sharp decline may reflect reduced service demand or successful cost-saving measures.

The overall trend shows an increase of £18.1M (62.9%) from 2017 to 2025. However, the year-over-year changes reveal a more complex picture, with spending rising and falling significantly in different years. This volatility may indicate that the council's spending is influenced by external factors, such as changes in central government funding or local economic conditions.

Supplier Diversity and Competition

With 4,614 unique suppliers over 10 years, West Lancashire Borough Council demonstrates a high level of supplier diversity. This diversity can promote competition and innovation, ensuring that the council gets the best value for its money. However, it also raises questions about supplier retention and the potential benefits of longer-term partnerships. Building strong relationships with key suppliers can lead to better terms, improved service quality, and reduced administrative costs.

The council may wish to analyse its supplier retention rates and identify opportunities to foster longer-term relationships with key vendors. This could involve reviewing its procurement processes, assessing supplier performance, and exploring opportunities for framework agreements or strategic partnerships. By striking a balance between supplier diversity and retention, the council can ensure that it gets the best value for its money while promoting competition and innovation.

What You Can Do

  • Submit a Freedom of Information request to West Lancashire Borough Council about its supplier retention rates and procurement strategy
  • Attend council committee meetings to learn more about its spending priorities and procurement processes
  • Check the council's published contracts register to see which suppliers it works with and the value of those contracts
  • Write to your local councillor about the importance of supplier diversity and retention in ensuring value for money
  • Visit aidoge.co.uk to explore the full spending data and analyse supplier patterns yourself
This article is based on West Lancashire Borough Council's published spending data covering 2016/17 to 2025/26. All figures are drawn directly from transactions published under the Local Government Transparency Code. Analysis by AI DOGE.
